title: bridge-slack: Slack events HTTP server binds 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces)
description: bridge-slack/src/bridge.rs:189: 'tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(format\!("0.0.0.0:{listen_port}"))' — binds to all network interfaces. The Slack events endpoint at /slack/events is supposed to receive webhooks from Slack only, not from LAN peers or other external sources. The signature verification (HMAC-SHA256) protects against unauthenticated requests, so this is not a direct exploit. However: (1) binding 0.0.0.0 unnecessarily exposes the port to LAN, (2) a firewall misconfiguration that passes this port through to the internet creates a DoS surface (anyone can send 1MB bodies repeatedly up to the DefaultBodyLimit). Best practice is to bind 127.0.0.1 and let a reverse proxy (nginx/caddy) forward Slack webhooks. Add a config option or default to 127.0.0.1.
